visa_policy.notes: The official e-visa portal (evisa.gov.ss) lists 207 nationalities. Two are marked EXEMPTED (visa-free with USD $0 fee): Egypt and Tanzania. All others require an e-visa applied through the online portal at accounts.eservices.gov.ss. The Embassy of South Sudan in Washington D.C. states that East African Community nationals and Egyptians are charged USD $0.00, which is consistent with Tanzania being EXEMPTED and Egypt being EXEMPTED; however, among other EAC members, Kenya, Uganda, and Rwanda show as requiring e-visas at the reduced fee of USD $50 in the evisa.gov.ss table, while Burundi is listed at the standard full rate of USD $100. South Sudanese nationals holding valid South Sudanese passports or Origin Cards do not require a visa. Diplomatic/official/service passport holders receive gratis visas. The e-visa portal offers: single entry (standard fee), 3-month multiple entry, and 6-month multiple entry options. Transit visas are valid for up to 72 hours. Visa validity begins on the date of issuance. The portal also includes a separate entry for 'Saint Christopher Cape' (likely a data-entry error or archaic variant; may overlap with Cabo Verde or Saint Kitts) at the standard USD $100 rate.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on any official South Sudan government source. Searches of mofaic.gov.ss, evisa.gov.ss, and other official domains returned no such program.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic, official, service and special passports issued to nationals of any country can obtain a visa on arrival. Foreign officials or diplomats must present an official Note Verbale from the appropriate authorities. Visas to diplomats are gratis (no visa fee assessed). Multiple-entry visas are only granted to diplomatic and official passports.

validation_notes: Re-validated 2026-06-13 against live evisa.gov.ss eligibility table (Firecrawl full-render scrape of 207 entries), mofaic.gov.ss/types-of-visas/, mofaic.gov.ss/visas/, and mofaic.gov.ss/visa-free-countries/.
CONFIRMED: Only Egypt and Tanzania are marked EXEMPTED (visa-free, USD $0); all other nationalities are shown as YES (e-visa required). No new EXEMPTED countries found.

CORRECTED: Previous notes incorrectly implied Burundi received a reduced EAC fee of USD $50; the live portal shows Burundi at the standard full rate of USD $100 (single entry). Only Kenya, Uganda, and Rwanda receive the USD $50 reduced rate among remaining EAC members.
CONFIRMED: United States e-visa fees are USD $160 single entry, USD $500 for 3-month multiple entry; 6-month multiple entry explicitly listed as 'Not Available' — consistent with existing record.

CONFIRMED: South Africa listed as 'South Africa (For less than 30 days stay)' with no fees displayed; Malaysia listed as 'Malaysia (For less than 30 days stay)' at standard USD $100 rates — consistent with existing record.

NEW FINDING: Live portal contains a new entry 'Saint Christopher Cape' at YES/USD 100 not previously documented. This is an ambiguous/erroneous entry (possibly Saint Kitts & Nevis, Cabo Verde, or a legacy name) and is not mapped to a JSON e-visa entry pending clarification.
CONFIRMED: No new conditional-access credential-based rules (US/Schengen visa holders, etc.) found on any official South Sudan source.
CONFIRMED: cbi, rbi, and fast_track remain null/empty — no programs found on official sources.
CONFIRMED: Diplomatic/official/service passport VoA rule from mofaic.gov.ss/types-of-visas/ still active and correctly recorded in conditional_access.
gaps: Max stay duration (days) for visa-free and e-visa entries is not stated on the official eligibility table; only the type and fee are listed.

The official eligibility table lists 207 nationalities but does not include South Sudan itself (nationals are exempt, confirmed separately). The table also includes entries like 'Darussalam', 'Kampuchea', 'Malagasy', 'Pacific Isles', 'Reunion', 'Salvador', 'Yugoslavia and former States of Yugoslavia' which are ambiguous or outdated country names — these were omitted from this JSON to avoid incorrect ISO3 assignments but exist in the official table.

EAC membership fee-waiver discrepancy: The Embassy of South Sudan DC states EAC nationals pay USD $0, but the live evisa.gov.ss portal (re-verified 2026-06-13) shows Kenya, Uganda, and Rwanda at the reduced rate of USD $50, while Burundi is listed at the full standard rate of USD $100 — Burundi does NOT receive the EAC reduced rate on the portal. Tanzania and Egypt are EXEMPTED (USD $0). The authoritative evisa portal data is used here.
No official information found for residency-by-investment, golden visa, fast-track skilled visa, startup visa, or digital-nomad visa programs on South Sudan official sources.

South Sudan signed the IGAD Free Movement Protocol but the protocol is not yet in force as an operational freedom-of-movement area; no official proclamation of a freedom-of-movement agreement was found on official government sources.
The table entry 'Greenland' (not a sovereign state) is listed as YES/USD 100 in the live portal; omitted from JSON as it is not a sovereign state with an ISO3 code.

The live portal (re-verified 2026-06-13) also includes a separate entry 'Saint Christopher Cape' at YES/USD 100 — this appears to be an erroneous or archaic entry (possibly conflating Saint Kitts and Nevis with Cabo Verde, or referencing an old name). It is not a recognized sovereign state ISO entry and is omitted from structured JSON.
South Sudan is a member of EAC (East African Community) but the official evisa portal does not reflect a blanket EAC visa-free policy; individual country entries are used instead.

Conditional access (Type A — third-country credential): No official South Sudan source (evisa.gov.ss, mofaic.gov.ss, ssembassydc.org, immigration.eservices.gov.ss) mentions any rule granting visa-free/VoA/e-visa access because a traveller holds a US, UK, Schengen, or other foreign visa or residence permit. No such credential-based conditional rule was found.
Conditional access (Type B — special status): No OCI cardholder or similar diaspora/heritage status entry rule found for South Sudan in any official source.

Conditional access (Type C — named bilateral diplomatic passport waivers): No named bilateral visa suppression/waiver agreement with specific partner countries and dates was found in any official source. The rule recorded is a blanket policy for 'any country' diplomatic/official/service/special passports per mofaic.gov.ss/types-of-visas/.

outbound_official.notes: The South Sudan Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation (mofaic.gov.ss) publishes a 'Visa-free countries' page and a 'Visas upon arrival' sub-page under Consular Services, but both pages render with zero country data — they are empty placeholder shells (last modified 2023-03-02 per metadata; re-verified 2026-06-14). No other South Sudan government domain (.gov.ss) was found to publish an authoritative outbound list of destinations where South Sudan ordinary-passport citizens can travel visa-free, visa-on-arrival, via e-visa, or via ETA. Rely on destination-side data.

sources_checked.description: Official South Sudan e-visa eligibility table — lists all 207 nationalities with visa type and fee
Official South Sudan e-visa portal homepage
Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation — Visa page
Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation — Homepage
Embassy of South Sudan in Washington D.C. — Visa services page (fee categories)
East African Community — confirmed 6 Partner States including South Sudan
Ministry of Foreign Affairs — Types of Visas page; confirms gratis VoA for diplomatic/official/service/special passport holders, Note Verbale requirement
Ministry of Foreign Affairs — Visa-free countries page; page rendered empty/no content retrieved
Ministry of Foreign Affairs — Visas upon arrival page; page rendered empty/no content retrieved
Department of E-Visa and Immigration Services portal; no conditional access rules found
South Sudan Passports and Immigration Act 2011 (Ministry of Justice); PDF unreadable (compressed binary)

Re-validation 2026-06-13: Full JavaScript-rendered scrape via Firecrawl confirmed all 207 entries, EXEMPTED status for Egypt and Tanzania only, fee amounts for all tiers, and Burundi at standard USD $100 (not reduced). New entry 'Saint Christopher Cape' also present.
Re-validation 2026-06-13: Confirmed diplomatic/official/service passport VoA gratis rule remains in effect. Note Verbale requirement confirmed.
Re-validation 2026-06-13: Page still renders empty — no substantive list content retrieved.
Re-validation 2026-06-13: Page still renders empty — no substantive content retrieved.
